text: Buses out of Splott stop at 18:00 so apprentices doing late shifts cannot get home. Two young people have dropped out of programmes this term because of it.
Families in Ely engage with the community partnership but not statutory services. Trust is the blocker, not awareness. Co-located outreach has worked where conventional referral has not.
Plenty of entry-level employability programmes in the city centre, very few that support the first six months in work. The cliff edge is at week 8, not at the door.
ESOL provision is full; refugees and asylum seekers wait 4+ months for a place. Without language progression, employability and integration support cannot land.
Young people with additional learning needs (ALN) hit the sharpest gap between school and post-16 — handoffs are slow, support is lost, and parents are left to navigate without a clear route.
